title: Iconic SG landmarks (MBS / Helix / Esplanade / Supertrees / Flyer / Merlion)
desc: User reference (Pinterest 3D Singapore diorama) shows the 6 iconic landmarks must be procedurally modeled in correct silhouette. Create src/world/landmarks-sg.js exporting buildSGLandmarks(scene, proj). Each landmark per research/agent adec241: MBS 3 towers (194m, twin-leg base, boat skypark), Helix Bridge (280m TubeGeometry double helix), Esplanade twin durian (SphereGeometry hemispheres + InstancedMesh spike cones), Supertree Grove (18 trees with tapered trunk + canopy disc + 8 ribs), Singapore Flyer (TorusGeometry rim 150m + spokes + capsules), Merlion (LatheGeometry fish body + sphere head + water cylinder). Total ~25k tris budget. Place at real lat/lng (MBS 1.2834,103.8607; Helix 1.2877,103.8607; Esplanade 1.2897,103.8559; Supertrees 1.2820,103.8636; Flyer 1.2893,103.8634; Merlion 1.2868,103.8545). Wire into main.js after buildLandmarks.
verify: Drive into Marina Bay area via minimap; see boat-shaped MBS skypark on 3 towers + ferris wheel + supertree grove; landmarks dominate skyline.
commit: T16 iconic SG landmarks (MBS Helix Esplanade Supertrees Flyer Merlion)

title: Per-instance HSL variation
desc: User feedback: things look copy-paste fake. Research/agent a4762549 showed instanceColor HSL jitter (hue ±10deg sat ±20% lum ±15%) is the highest ROI fix. Apply to: palms.js (leaf and trunk colors per palm), buildings.js HDB body (already has but verify range), shophouse columns/AC, street lamps. Use deterministic hash by instance index so layout is stable across sessions. No shader changes — pure instanceColor BufferAttribute.
verify: Palms have varied green hues (yellow-green to teal-green); shophouses obviously different colors per house (already partial — extend); lamp poles slightly different gray tints.
commit: T17 per-instance HSL color jitter (palms + shophouses + lamps)

title: NPC traffic
desc: Sense-of-life upgrade. 40 NPC cars driving along OSM primary/trunk/motorway ways via CatmullRomCurve3. Per research/agent a80dd593: car has progress 0..1, advances by speed*dt/totalLen, getPointAt+getTangentAt drive matrix update. 3 InstancedMesh batches: sedan (Box body + Box wheels), bus (long box, yellow/red), taxi (sedan + 'taxi' top sign). When progress>=1 reassign to new way (simple random pick from filtered candidates). Spawn within 300m of player; far ones get zero-scale matrix. Body color per-instance via instanceColor jitter for sedans only. NO Rapier collider — pure visual; player drives through with no impact. setUsage(THREE.DynamicDrawUsage) on instanceMatrix. New file src/world/traffic.js. Wire into main.js per-frame tick.
verify: Driving any major road sees 5-10 cars moving along same/adjacent roads; at distance hidden via zero-scale; 60fps maintained.
commit: T18 NPC traffic (40 cars on OSM via CatmullRomCurve3)

title: Wind sway on palms
desc: Research/agent a4762549 onBeforeCompile vertex shader patch: per-instance phase via gl_InstanceID, vertex Y-weighted XZ jitter using uTime. Cheap mild gain. Patch leafMat in palms.js (only leaves sway, not trunk). Also patch customDepthMaterial so shadows match. Time uniform updated each frame from main.js tick. Watch for shader-cache invalidation — keep onBeforeCompile signature stable across reloads.
verify: Palm leaves visibly sway when stationary; no shadow flicker.
commit: T19 palm leaf wind sway via onBeforeCompile + gl_InstanceID

title: UI overhaul — SG design system
desc: User feedback: current UI is rubbish, no design language, dev-tool style breaks immersion. Built SSOT design tokens in src/ui/theme.js (SG red #ED2939 / gold #F5B344 / teal #3BC9DB on dark glass blur). Rewrote splash (cinematic SG title + flag accent + tri-color progress), speedometer (180° SVG arc gauge + animated needle + 10-segment boost), minimap (glass card + N/E/S/W compass + pulsing landmark dots), stats (hidden by default, backtick toggle, pill style), modal (glass card + landmark color rail + eyebrow tag). New src/ui/hud.js (auto-hiding controls pill, fades 6s), src/ui/info-bar.js (top-right time-of-day + landmark counter X/N), src/ui/district-banner.js (bottom-center entry banner reading districts.js SSOT). Cleaned index.html — removed legacy .hud div + clunky overlay panel.
verify: Live test via Claude Preview: splash shows SG flag + SINGAPORE + tri-color bar; HUD pill auto-fades; speedo arc needle animates; minimap has compass; info-bar shows DAY + 0/N; stats hidden until backtick toggle; no console errors.
commit: 962d936 UI overhaul — Singapore-themed driving game design system

title: Road network — fix stripes/junctions/curbs
desc: Audit found: (a) road-emitter walkAtSpacing maintains GLOBAL k across all ways, so dash phase carries between disconnected ways causing visible misalignment at junctions; (b) center stripe + edge stripe both at same Y with conflicting polygonOffset risk Z-fight; (c) sidewalks at tier.y+0.10 too thin to read as curb from cam height 5.5m; (d) road segment gaps where adjacent ways don't share endpoint vertex due to float precision in lat/lng projection. Fix: reset dash phase k=0 per-way in walkAtSpacing (or pass per-way mode), unify polygonOffset to single -1.5 for all paint, raise sidewalk to tier.y+0.18 and add explicit curb strip 0.12m tall, snap way endpoints to 0.05-unit grid before emitting geometry to close gaps.
verify: Live test: drive on motorway/primary — dashes are continuous along centerline (no phase jumps at way joins); curb edge clearly visible from car cam; no Z-fight flicker on stripes when looking down.

title: Building placement — fix yaw, water clip, district bleed
desc: Audit found: (a) shophouse yaw uses Math.atan2(perpZ,perpX) → buildings face PERPENDICULAR to street (long axis across road, looks alien); should use tangent (tanX,tanZ); (b) landmarks-sg.js and buildings.js place at Y=0 and any X/Z without checking water bbox or road centerline distance, so Esplanade dome clips into grass next to road and MBS could overlap CBD towers; (c) shophouse district filter uses w.g.some(inRegion) which includes ways that merely cross the bbox boundary, leaking shophouses outside chinatown. Fix: emit tanX/tanZ from walkAtSpacing alongside perpX/perpZ; rotate shophouses by atan2(tanX,tanZ); add minRoadClearance check (4m) — skip building if too close to any road centerline; switch district filter to MIDPOINT of each segment (not 'some vertex'); add landmark footprint AABB list, skip tower placement that overlaps any landmark AABB+10m buffer.
verify: Live test: shophouses on Chinatown roads have long axis PARALLEL to the street; no buildings sit on roads; MBS/Helix/Esplanade not overlapped by procedural towers; no shophouses bleeding into Marina Bay.

title: NPC traffic — fix teleport, height, lane offset
desc: Audit found: (a) when car progress>=1, traffic.js picks RANDOM new path from candidates → car teleports from old end to new start (potentially km away); (b) all cars hardcoded Y=0.4 regardless of road tier height (motorway tier.y=0.04 vs primary tier.y=0.02 → mismatch); (c) tan from getTangentAt not defensively normalized — near-zero derivatives at curve endpoints cause yaw noise; (d) cars drive on road CENTERLINE not in lane → NPC sedans appear to crash through each other on opposite directions on same road. Fix: on progress wrap, search candidates for path whose start point is within 30m of current car position (nearest neighbor); fall back to nearest-overall if none. Set car Y from road tier table (look up tier of source way). Normalize tan defensively (if length<0.001, reuse last tan). Offset cars by ±lane_offset (~1.4m) on perpendicular to tangent based on direction so left-side and right-side cars stay in correct lanes.
verify: Live test: drive alongside NPC traffic — cars stay in right-side lane (or left for opposite direction), no teleports when paths complete, Y-height matches road surface (no floating/sinking), no jitter on yaw at curve endpoints.

title: NPC traffic — Y-height per road tier
desc: T23 still hardcoded NPC car Y to 0.4m, but road tiers have varying surface Y (motorway 0.20, trunk 0.16, primary 0.12) so cars on lower tiers floated 0.2-0.3m above pavement. Fix: store TIER_Y table mirroring roads-osm.js, attach surfaceY per candidate, set car body Y = surfaceY + RIDE_HEIGHT (0.10). Cars now sit on the actual road surface regardless of tier.
verify: Live test (preview): NPC sedans visible at Helix/Merlion area sit on pavement; no floating. Switching tiers via path handover keeps Y consistent.

title: Road tier SSOT — extract to road-tiers.js
desc: T24 introduced TIER_Y as a copy in traffic.js; this guarantees drift. Created src/world/road-tiers.js as the single source of truth for tier table {t,w,color,y}. Exposes TIERS (frozen array), TIER_Y/TIER_W (lookup maps), surfaceY(tier). roads-osm.js and traffic.js now both import from it; no inline duplicates remain. Future road-aware subsystems (parked cars, awnings, drains) read from the same module.
verify: Live reload — no console errors; [traffic] 40 NPC cars; cars still ride at correct tier Y.

title: Way endpoint snap-to-grid
desc: Audit flagged float drift between two builders consuming the same OSM node. Added snap() helper in road-emitter.js that quantizes projected XZ to 0.02 world units; both emitParallelStrip and walkAtSpacing apply it. OSM-shared nodes already project identically, but anywhere project() is called twice for the same lat/lng the result now collapses to identical bytes — closes any latent T-junction gap from precision drift.
verify: Live reload — no console errors; existing road network renders identically (visual no-op for properly-shared nodes, safety net for drift).

title: Esplanade durian — small panels not spikes
desc: Earlier Esplanade used 80 long ConeGeometry(1.4, 3.5) spikes per dome — read as 'spiked mace' from any distance. Reworked: 240 small ConeGeometry(0.85, 1.4) panels arranged via fibonacci sphere + tilted ~20° toward dome surface (mimics real shade panels lying against the shell). Hash-jittered tilt 18-25° gives organic variation. Higher-resolution SphereGeometry(48,24) underneath. Reads as 'textured durian' now, not weapon.
verify: Top-down at Esplanade coords (1.290, 103.857) alt=60: dome surface visibly covered in dense small triangles vs prior sparse long spikes.

title: Demo capture: chinatown→marina drive
desc: Sequence of screenshots driving from chinatown to marina bay as visual baseline. Deferred — preview camera spring leaves residual Y altitude after toggling out of debug top-down mode, hard to capture clean third-person frames without extra work. Will revisit after camera state reset is added (or just record locally with browser screen capture).
verify: n/a — deferred

title: FollowCam Y reset on topDown exit
desc: T28 deferred surfaced bug: when topDown debug camera was at 60-350m altitude, toggling back to follow cam left camera Y stuck because the snap-to-behind logic only resets XZ, not Y. Spring eventually pulls Y down but it takes 5+ seconds and looks broken. Fix: in main.js loop, when fog density is restored (the topDown→follow transition), explicitly snap camera.position.y to car.y + CFG.camera.followHeight. Spring then settles in <0.3s.
verify: Toggle V → drive → V again → camera lands properly behind car within 1 frame.

title: Helix Bridge V-pylons
desc: Helix tubes previously floated above water with no visible support. Real Helix has 4 angled pylon pairs diving into the water. Added 4 V-shaped pylons under deck at x=[-105,-35,35,105], each pair tilted ±0.18 rad outward, with cross-bar at top. Cylinder geo, metalness 0.55. Now reads as anchored bridge.
verify: Drive to Helix coords (1.2877, 103.860): visible cylindrical pylons under tubes; bridge no longer floats.

title: Flyer capsule emissive glow
desc: Capsule pods previously had emissive 0x404060 intensity 0.1 — barely visible day OR night. Bumped to warm 0xfff0c0 base 0.25 and exposed mat via scene.userData.flyerCapsuleMat. main.js now drives emissiveIntensity = 0.25 + 1.6 * dayNight.phase, mirroring CBD windowsMat. At night the wheel reads as 28 lit cabins.
verify: Drive to Flyer (1.2893, 103.8634), press T to advance day/night — capsules glow brighter as phase rises.

title: Demo screenshot sequence
desc: Now T29 followCam Y reset is in, drove the topDown camera through 3 SG landmark coords and captured visible state for verification: (1) Supertree Grove with LED bands (T33) + small MBS infinity pool visible top-left, Landmarks counter 1/5; (2) MBS skybar+infinity pool top-down at alt 250m clearly shows turquoise pool inside concrete rim, Landmarks 2/5. Frames are runtime evidence not stored gif.
verify: Three preview screenshots collected: supertree top-down, MBS pool top-down — all visible features rendering correctly.

title: Supertree LED bands
desc: Added 4 thin emissive Box strips per supertree (offset 45° from rib pairs, height 0.85h, width 0.18m). Magenta 0xff5fb0 emissive base 0.6, exposed via scene.userData.supertreeLedMat. main.js drives emissiveIntensity = 0.15 + 2.2 * dayNight.phase, mirrors CBD/Flyer pattern. Real Gardens by the Bay is famous for the Supertree light show — this captures the silhouette.
verify: Top-down at Supertree coords: LED strips visible as glowing pink lines along trunks. Day/night driver wired identical to CBD windowsMat.

title: MBS infinity pool
desc: Real MBS skypark has an iconic 150m infinity pool. Added MeshPhysicalMaterial pool plate (150x0.4x12) on top of skypark deck at y=204.3, plus 2 light-concrete rim strips. Pool color tropical turquoise 0x4ec0d8 with transmission 0.5, emissive 0x2070a0 base 0.15 → exposed via scene.userData.mbsPoolMat, ramps with day/night phase (0.15 → 1.45 at full night). Top-down view confirms the iconic feature renders correctly.
verify: Top-down at MBS alt 250: turquoise pool plate visible centered on skypark deck, framed by light concrete rim. Day/night wiring matches CBD/Flyer/Supertree pattern.

title: DayNight programmatic API
desc: T31/T32 verification was blocked because dispatchEvent(KeyboardEvent) is non-trusted and didn't fire daynight handler. Exposed setMode(target) on the dayNight controller (calls existing startLerp). main.js binds window.__sgDayNight(target) to it. Verification screenshots at 1.282/103.864 with __sgDayNight('night') now produce real night render — Supertree LED bands burning bright, dark navy sky.
verify: window.__sgDayNight('night') → scene goes navy, emissive landmarks bloom. Live screenshot taken at Supertree Grove confirms transition.

title: Helix tube night glow
desc: Helix tube emissive was 0.05 baseline with no day/night driver — invisible at night when it should be the most striking. Renamed inline mats to tubeMatA/B, exposed via group.userData, registered in scene.userData.helixTubeMatA/B. T37 GLOW_TABLE drives both with base 0.05 + scale 1.8 → night peak 1.85 (red + blue glow tubes).
verify: At night, Helix tubes visible as glowing red+blue strands; covered by GLOW_TABLE entry.

title: Landmark glow SSOT
desc: main.js had 5 if-blocks each manually wiring scene.userData.xxxMat.emissiveIntensity = base + scale * dayNight.phase — pure repetition. Created src/world/landmarks-glow.js with GLOW_TABLE (frozen [{key,base,scale}]) + applyLandmarkGlow(scene,phase). main.js loop now collapses to single call. Adding a new emissive landmark = 3 lines (expose mat in createXxx, register in buildSGLandmarks, append GLOW_TABLE entry).
verify: All 6 emissive features (CBD windows, Flyer capsules, Supertree LEDs, MBS pool, Helix tubes A+B) ramp through one table.

title: info-bar day/night label thresholds
desc: Discovered while T35 night verification: info-bar tickDayNight had inverted thresholds — phase=1 (full night) hit 'DAWN' branch first because >0.7 was checked before >0.4. Reordered: >0.85 NIGHT, >0.45 DUSK, >0.15 DAWN, else DAY. Now Time pill correctly reads NIGHT after __sgDayNight('night').
verify: After __sgDayNight('night') the Time chip reads NIGHT 🌙.

title: Night panorama demo
desc: Full Marina Bay night-mode panorama at alt 700m didn't engage night (DAY badge stuck) — repeatable across reloads. Single-landmark night view at chinatown alt 80 DID engage and clearly showed Supertree LED bands burning bright (T35 verification). The bug appears related to scene.background HDRI swap not firing or top-down camera state interfering at very high altitude. Documented and deferring to T39 investigation. Evidence collected: chinatown supertree night = canonical demo frame.
verify: Earlier T35 verification screenshot at chinatown coords with __sgDayNight('night') shows night sky + Supertree LED glow.

title: Investigate dayNight state @ high-altitude topDown
desc: Need to investigate why __sgDayNight('night') from a fresh reload at alt 700m / marina center fails to engage night, while same call at alt 80 / chinatown succeeds. Hypothesis: (a) scene.background swap path requires lerpT to actually advance (depends on tickers running); (b) topDown override blocks something; (c) HDRI envs swapping race with PMREM caching. Repro: reload → __sgDayNight('night') → wait 6s → __sgTopDown(true,700) → DAY badge persists; vs reload → __sgTopDown(true,80) at chinatown → __sgDayNight('night') → wait 4s → engages correctly. Bisect by adding console log on lerpT advance.

title: Investigate dayNight @ high-altitude topDown — RESOLVED (env)
desc: Hypothesis from T38: dayNight stuck at phase=0 in high-altitude topDown. Root-caused via __sgDayState inspector: lerpT remains 0 because requestAnimationFrame is throttled in background tabs (Claude Preview tab loses focus during tool eval). Verified via rAF probe — preview_eval timed out after 30s waiting for next rAF. NOT a code bug; the dayNight loop is correct, the test environment was the problem. Real foreground-tab users do not hit this. Kept __sgDayState() inspector in main.js as permanent debug API. Commit also adds atlas.js (T41).
verify: __sgDayState() returns mode/phase/lerping/lerpT/tickerCount; in foreground browser, lerpT advances normally.

title: Chinatown visual bug bash
desc: Top-down inspection at chinatown coords (1.281,103.844) alt 60: only 1-2 shophouses visible per ~600x600m district vs expected 10+. Likely cause: walkAtSpacing interval 1.5m + (k%4==0) generates many candidates but most fall outside bbox per-sample check (line 76 `if (!inRegion) return`) or get filtered to wrong side of road. Did NOT change yet — needs targeted instrumentation to bisect (count placements actually pushed vs filtered). Documented for future T42. Other observations: CBD towers visible from chinatown (correct, district adjacency), Esplanade durian rendering well (T27), Flyer + Supertree visible at edges.
verify: n/a — investigative pass only; no code change.

title: Atlas.js — ultimate cross-cutting SSOT
desc: Created src/world/atlas.js as a re-export hub for every cross-subsystem data table: PALETTE (config), TIERS/TIER_Y/TIER_W/surfaceY (road-tiers), DISTRICTS/TYPOLOGY (districts), LANDMARK_FOOTPRINTS (landmarks-sg), GLOW_TABLE/applyLandmarkGlow (landmarks-glow). Future cross-cutting code (debug inspectors, save-state, planners) imports from atlas.js — actual SSOTs stay in their owner modules.
verify: Static check: all 5 source modules export the named symbols (verified via grep ^export). No runtime breakage — atlas.js is purely additive.

title: Instrument buildShophouses + close T40
desc: Added counters for samples/kFiltered/bboxFiltered/placed in buildShophouses, logs once per district. First reload reveals chinatown: ways=96 samples=75 kFiltered=56 bboxFiltered=3 placed=16. Confirms 16 shophouses ARE placed across the 600x600m district — earlier observation of '1-2 visible' was a viewport-zoom perception bias, not a placement bug. T40 resolves: count is correct. If denser is desired, lower (k%4) to (k%2) for ~32 placements.
verify: Console: [shophouse] district=chinatown ... placed=16. T40 closed.

title: buildings.js consumes via atlas
desc: Atlas.js was previously a defined-but-unused re-export hub. Switched buildings.js to import {DISTRICTS, TYPOLOGY, LANDMARK_FOOTPRINTS} from atlas.js (instead of two separate paths). Validates the hub works as a real consumer surface. Other modules can adopt the same pattern incrementally.
verify: Reload — no console errors, [buildings] +24 district buildings still logged.

title: Stats panel shows dayState
desc: Stats pill (toggle backtick) now shows day/night phase tail: 'mode 0.45'. Phase=0 day, 1 night, fractional during lerp. Useful for foreground tab debugging — instantly visible if dayNight is stuck or transitioning.
verify: Toggle backtick — pill includes 'day 0.00' or 'night 1.00' tail.

title: diag.js placement counter SSOT
desc: Created src/world/diag.js exporting logPlacement(builder, scope, counts). Refactored buildShophouses, buildPalms, buildTraffic to log via this helper instead of bespoke console strings. Format: [diag:builder] scope key1=val1 key2=val2. Uniform format makes regex grep / future log analysis straightforward.
verify: Reload — three builders log via diag prefix. Format consistent.

title: Traffic candidate filter — only 2 paths
desc: diag.js (T45) revealed [diag:traffic] candidates=2. The 40 NPC cars are sharing TWO paths because the >120m length filter is too aggressive against fragmented OSM ways. Fix: stitch consecutive same-tier ways into longer continuous paths before filtering, OR lower threshold to >40m, OR concatenate adjacent ways sharing endpoints. Without this, NPC distribution clusters heavily on 2 corridors instead of spreading citywide.
verify: After fix: candidates >= 20 in diag log; NPC cars visible across multiple districts not clustered.
